summary: Added a hosted macOS CI runner hygiene step that untaps the preinstalled aws/tap before installing native Homebrew Swift tooling, using the Homebrew tap-trust override only for that cleanup command so the normal swiftformat/swiftlint install step runs after the unrelated tap is removed.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS CI removes the hosted-runner aws/tap before installing swiftformat and swiftlint.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml adds the named step Remove hosted CI runner aws/tap immediately before brew install swiftformat swiftlint.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The hygiene behavior is scoped to CI runner state, not local Mac setup.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: The step name and evidence/m4-macos-ci-brew-tap-hygiene/notes.md describe this as hosted CI runner hygiene.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS remains native and Linux CI behavior is unchanged.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: The macOS job still uses jdx/mise-action, Homebrew, and just macos-check; the linux job was not modified.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Repository checks pass after the workflow edit.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: nix develop -c just check and git diff --check passed; see evidence/m4-macos-ci-brew-tap-hygiene/commands.log.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Hosted GitHub Actions run 28715640440 passed on macOS and the filtered macOS job log showed aws/tap was untapped before `brew install swiftformat swiftlint`, with no `not trusted` or `Tap-Trust` annotation in the install step.
